prompt: What is the primary purpose of a self-service portal in ITSM?
choices: To give users a single place to submit requests, report issues, and track work status without contacting the service desk directly
To replace all assignment groups with one global queue
To disable incident categorization and prioritization
To run background scripts for platform upgrades
explanation: A self-service portal improves user experience and service desk efficiency by centralizing access to IT services and request visibility.

prompt: How does Employee Service Center support ITSM outcomes?
choices: It unifies employee-facing access to IT services, knowledge, and request workflows in a modern portal experience
It automatically marks all incidents as major incidents
It removes the need for service catalog governance
It is used only for CMDB relationship mapping
explanation: Employee Service Center helps streamline intake and self-service by presenting relevant services and knowledge in a single employee experience.

prompt: What is a key best practice when exposing catalog items in Employee Service Center?
choices: Use clear taxonomy and audience-appropriate content so users can quickly find the right service and submit complete requests
Show every internal fulfillment task to all end users
Remove all variable help text to reduce page length
Require users to open separate incidents before every request
explanation: A well-organized catalog with understandable content reduces misrouted requests and improves fulfillment speed and data quality.

prompt: How does a knowledge portal reduce incident volume in ITSM?
choices: By enabling users to resolve common issues through searchable, reusable articles before submitting tickets
By disabling incident creation for all users
By routing every request directly to change management
By replacing SLA policies with article ratings
explanation: Effective knowledge access increases self-resolution and deflects repetitive tickets, allowing agents to focus on higher-complexity work.

prompt: Which metric most directly indicates that portal and knowledge experiences are improving ITSM performance?
choices: Increased self-service resolution and reduced repeat incidents for known issues
Higher number of mandatory approval steps per request
More assignment group transfers per ticket
Longer average time to publish knowledge articles
explanation: When users can find and use services and knowledge effectively, organizations typically see more self-resolution and fewer repeat contacts.
